Q5 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • INSTRUMENTATION ENGINEERING • Beginner • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

How many three digit numbers can be generated from 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 such that the digits are in ascending order?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
If the first digit of the three digit number is 1 and the second digit is 2, then the last digit can be
filled in 7 ways (either by 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9).
Similarly, if second digit is 3, then the last digit can be filled in 6 ways and so on.
The same method is applicable for digit 2, 3, _ _ _ _ 7 at the first place. Hence, the total three digit numbers with digits in ascending order,
= (7 + 6 + 5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 1) + (6 + 5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 1) + _ _ __ _ _ _ _ + (2 + 1) + 1
= 28 + 21 + 15 + 10 + 6 + 3 + 1 = 84

Q15 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following pseudo code:

Which of the following is the output of the program if dynamic scoping is used?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
In dynamic scoping, variable value of closest activation record is used. Here, procedure of activation record is like
1. main( ),
2. show( ), global value of r is closest, so print 0.25, and terminated, so back to main( )
3. small( ), r = 0.125, show( ), here value of r in small function is closest to show, so print 0.125,
return to small( ), then return to main( ).
4. Terminate main( ).

Q16 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider two transactions T1 and T2 with timestamp 100 and 110 respectively.
It says T2 started after T1.
Let T2 reads a data D. So, read timestamp of D is 110 i.e. Read_TS(D) = 110.
Now, T1 issues a write operation on data D.
Following are some outputs:
S1 : If timestamps protocols is used then system rejects this write operation and rolls T1 back.
S2 : If Thomas Write rule is used then system will ignore this write operation of T1.
Which of the above outputs are correct?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a)
In this situation conflict is read_write. Here r2(D) is reading wrong value.
So, T1 should be rolled back in both protocols.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Match the following port number with their uses:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Port 20 and 21 used for FTP.
• Port 80 used for HTTP.
• Port 23 used for Telnet.
• Port 123 used for NTP (Network Time Protocol).
• Network Time Protocol (NTP) uses UDP port 123 for all time synchronization.

Q25 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are incorrect regarding cache memory?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
(a) Reducing the compulsory misses using increasing block size is based on principle of spatial locality.
(c) Conflict misses reduce on increasing the associativity.
(d) Writing to memory require much more cycle, so it is preferable that write to buffer first then memory.

Q27 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Relation R has five attributes {A, B, C, D, E}. Following are functional dependencies of set F for R:
A → B, C → D, AC → BC, BD → E
Which of the following is/are correct for R?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• Composition rule: A → B, C → D, so AC → BD and BD → E. So, AC → E using transitivity. And (b) is incorrect.
• A → B, using augmentation rule, AC → BC. So, AC → BC can be implied by F – {AC → BC}.

Q29 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

TCP client P successfully establishes a connection to TCP server Q. Let NP denote the sequence number in the SYN sent from P to Q. Let NQ denote the acknowledgement number in the SYN ACK from Q to P. Which of the following statements is/are CORRECT?

Correct answer: C, D
Solution

(c, d)
The acknowledgment number will always be sequence number of the next expected data. The initial sequence number will always be random number within range of 0 to 232 – 1.

Not attempted

Given a singly linked list of n nodes where n is not known, and its header node is given. Which of the following is correct to find its middle node

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Use two pointer. First pointer will move two steps, and second will move only single step in one iteration of the loop. And stop the iteration when first pointer reaches the end. According to even or odd value of n, find the middle node using second pointer.

Which of the following address spaces are potential candidates from which the ISP can allot any one of the organization?
I. 202.61.84.0/21
II. 202.61.104.0/21
III. 202.61.64.0/21
IV. 202.61.144.0/21

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q41 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are incorrect statement/s regarding Heap tree and Binary Search Tree (BST)?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
A single node Max Heap or Min Heap can be a BST.
Conversion from BST to Max or Min Heap is similar to building Heap from given elements. Here get the elements from BST in O(n) and then build a Heap in θ(n log n).
Heap tree takes Ο(n) time to search an element. If BST is skew then search of element takes Ο(n) time.

Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66

An instruction pipeline has five stages, namely, instruction fetch (IF), instruction decode and register fetch (ID/RF), instruction execution (EX), memory access (MEM), and register writeback (WB).
Let following instructions sequence execute on this pipeline:

Which of the following is incorrect regarding DATA hazards arises on execution of above instructions on this pipeline?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
On executing instruction (iv) the data of R2 will be available in register just after finishing the stage WB of instruction (i). So, no forwarding required here. In other cases forwarding either from EX or MEM stage is required.
